parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report with significant editorial framing. The author's own prose consistently defends Common Core State Standards as evidence-based and sound, while dismissing critics through selective framing and editorial language. The piece aligns with a center-right, federalist, standards-based reform perspective that valorizes state autonomy over federal mandates — a position associated with establishment conservative education reformers. The accumulation of multiple directional framing choices warrants a Lean Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's own framing immediately sets up anti-Common Core critic Sandra Stotsky as the foil, using her position as a straw man to rebut rather than engaging her claims neutrally — the structure of the piece is argumentative, not balanced; (2) The author describes the move to repeal Common Core as 'ill-advised' in their own connecting prose, an editorial judgment that dismisses a policy position held by many conservative and progressive critics alike; (3) The author invokes Congressman John Kline, a Republican, and frames his endorsement of the ESSA as a 'huge win for conservatives' — by choosing to foreground this conservative endorsement prominently, the author signals alignment with establishment Republican education policy; (4) The author describes repeal efforts as producing only two outcomes — reaffirmation under a new name or 'inferior standards' — framing repeal advocates as either deceptive or wrong, with no neutral presentation of their substantive policy arguments; (5) The author characterizes Stotsky's critique of state revisions as a mere 'strategy du jour' used to 'deceive,' amplifying a dismissive framing of Common Core critics without offering a counterbalancing presentation of their case; (6) The piece relies heavily on sources from the Collaborative for Student Success and the Fordham Institute, both of which are center-right education reform organizations, with no counterbalancing sources critical of Common Core from either left or right.
